title: 'You've got to have some sort of credentials' — Aug 2019 leadership-and-learning aphorisms
html:
TL;DR

  • Five-line aphorism stack from Aug 15, 2019 (~3 weeks before junior year starts). Title is "You've got to have some sort of credentials" but the body never returns to credentials — the title is a sixth, top-level frame the rest of the note implicitly serves.
  • The five lines: "The CEOs job isn't to make a decision it is to make the right decision" / "When faced with a problem, just ask other people" / "Don't specialize to early" / "You have to be force to be in a situation to learn from it…. Chinese" / "Follow up and don't leave a void". A complete teen-CEO operating system in five bullets.
  • Genesis document for dont specialize too early (concept page already exists and cites this note) and a key source for leader as network not iq. The "ask other people" line is the network-as-cognition move that becomes the "surrounds himself with the smartest" thesis 9 weeks later.
  • The "forced into a situation to learn — Chinese" line is the involuntary-immersion thesis: language acquisition (and by extension all skill acquisition) requires constraint, not optionality. This becomes the explicit motivation for the shanghai study abroad choice 5 years later.
  • "Follow up and don't leave a void" is the politeness-as-protocol rule that surfaces operationally in the 2026 Rankett sales scripts and meetup follow-ups. Origin date now confirmed: Aug 2019.

Key quotes

"The CEOs job isn't to make a decision it is to make the right decision"

"Don't specialize to early"

"You have to be force to be in a situation to learn from it…. Chinese"

"Follow up and don't leave a void"

Why it matters in the graph

  • The single densest aphorism stack of the 2019 corpus. Five lines, four of which become real concepts (leader as network not iq, dont specialize too early, shanghai study abroad motivation, follow-up protocol). One note, four threads going forward.
  • The mismatched title is itself a Yahir signal — "credentials" was the surface anxiety, but the body shows the actual thinking was about leadership-style and learning-mechanism. Title-mismatch is chronic (2019 11 13 coding zeal is the most extreme case), and it's diagnostic: when title and body diverge, the body is what was alive in his head; the title is what he thought he was supposed to be working on.
  • The CEO-decision reframe ("isn't to make a decision, it is to make the right decision") is structurally the same as the asking the right question later concept — same meta-level move applied to the standard frame shape.

Open questions

  • Source attribution? "CEO makes the right decision" sounds like Maxwell or Jim Collins; "don't specialize too early" echoes David Epstein's Range (which dropped May 2019 — chronologically possible). Worth checking what books were in the "Read books" note from 9 days earlier.
  • Does the "follow up and don't leave a void" rule show up in any failures-to-follow-up regret notes later?

Wikilinks

dont specialize too early · leader as network not iq · shanghai study abroad · asking the right question · fear of not doing · career archetypes spy leader creator · john maxwell
